original_theorem: Let $X_1, \dots, X_n$ be a sequence of independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) random variables from a Bernoulli distribution with parameter $p$, where $0 < p < 1$. Let the sample mean be defined as $\bar{X}_n = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n X_i$. Determine the expected value of the sample mean, $E[\bar{X}_n]$, the variance of the sample mean, $Var(\bar{X}_n)$, and the limit of the variance as $n \to \infty$.
theorem_translation:
```lean4
theorem bernoulli_sample_mean_properties
    -- Let Ω be a probability space.
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace Ω] (ℙ : MeasureTheory.Measure Ω) [ProbabilityTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure ℙ]
    -- Let p be the success probability of the Bernoulli distribution.
    {p : ℝ} (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_lt_1 : p < 1)
    -- Let X₁, X₂, ... be a sequence of random variables.
    (X : ℕ → Ω → Fin 2)
    -- Assume the random variables are independent.
    (hindep : ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun (fun _ => inferInstance) X ℙ)
    -- Assume the random variables are identically distributed as Bernoulli(p).
    (hdist : ∀ i, ProbabilityTheory.HasDistrib (X i) (ProbabilityTheory.bernoulli p) ℙ) :
    let sample_mean (n : ℕ) := (fun ω ↦ (n : ℝ)⁻¹ * (∑ i in Finset.Icc 1 n, (X i ω : ℝ))) in
    (∀ n > 0, ProbabilityTheory.integral ℙ (sample_mean n) = p) ∧
    (∀ n > 0, ProbabilityTheory.variance ℙ (sample_mean n) = p * (1 - p) / (n : ℝ)) ∧
    (Filter.Tendsto (fun n ↦ ProbabilityTheory.variance ℙ (sample_mean n)) Filter.atTop (𝓝 0)) := by
```

theorem bernoulli_sample_mean_properties
    -- Let Ω be a probability space.
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace Ω] [ProbabilityTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : MeasureTheory.Measure Ω)]
    -- Let p be the success probability of the Bernoulli distribution.
    {p : ℝ} (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_lt_1 : p < 1)

    (X : ℕ → Ω → Fin 2)
    -- Assume the random variables are independent.

    -- Assume the random variables are identically distributed as Bernoulli(p).

    -- Let X_real be the real-valued version of the random variables.
    let X_real i ω := (X i ω : ℝ)
    -- Let sample_mean be the sample mean of the first n variables.
    let sample_mean (n : ℕ) := (n : ℝ)⁻¹ * (∑ i in Finset.Icc 1 n, X_real i) in

    (∀ (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n), ProbabilityTheory.integral ℙ (sample_mean n) = p) ∧
    (∀ (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n), ProbabilityTheory.variance ℙ (sample_mean n) = p * (1 - p) / n) ∧

import Mathlib
import Aesop

set_option maxHeartbeats 0
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat Filter
theorem bernoulli_sample_mean_properties
    -- Let Ω be a probability space.
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace Ω] (ℙ : MeasureTheory.Measure Ω) [ProbabilityTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure ℙ]
    -- Let p be the success probability of the Bernoulli distribution.
    {p : ℝ} (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_lt_1 : p < 1)
    -- Let X₁, X₂, ... be a sequence of random variables.
    (X : ℕ → Ω → Fin 2)
    -- Assume the random variables are independent.
    (hindep : ProbabilityTheory.iIndepFun (fun _ => inferInstance) X ℙ)
    -- Assume the random variables are identically distributed as Bernoulli(p).
    (hdist : ∀ i, ProbabilityTheory.HasDistrib (X i) (ProbabilityTheory.bernoulli p) ℙ) :
    let sample_mean (n : ℕ) := (fun ω ↦ (n : ℝ)⁻¹ * (∑ i in Finset.Icc 1 n, (X i ω : ℝ))) in
    (∀ n > 0, ProbabilityTheory.integral ℙ (sample_mean n) = p) ∧
    (∀ n > 0, ProbabilityTheory.variance ℙ (sample_mean n) = p * (1 - p) / (n : ℝ)) ∧
    (Filter.Tendsto (fun n ↦ ProbabilityTheory.variance ℙ (sample_mean n)) Filter.atTop (𝓝 0)) := by
